summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/spl-token exfiltrates host info and executes shell commands at install
details: Package name is a 1-character omission of the legitimate @solana-labs/spl-token. The package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs automatically on `npm install`. install.js imports child_process, fs, https, and os; collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(); reads platform info via process.platform; checks for files via fs.existsSync; and POSTs data over HTTPS. install.js also invokes execSync('powershell...') and execSync('curl...') to run platform-specific shell commands. Combined with the typosquatted scope/name targeting Solana developers, this is an install-time host-reconnaissance and command-execution payload triggered automatically on default install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
